rulings.Case Title: JASON ROUSSOS VS. POUR GUYS, INC. ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion For An Independent Psychiatric And Psychological Examination
rulings.Rulings: DEFENDANT POUR GUYS SOMA LLC DBA TEMPEST BAR & GRILL Notice Of Motion For An Independent Psychiatric And Psychological Examination (part 1 of 2) Pro Tem Judge Dianne Peebles, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Defendant Pour Guys Soma LLC's Notice Of Motion and Motion For An Independent Psychiatric And Psychological Examination and Plaintiff Jason Roussos's Opposition thereto having duly been considered it is hereby ORDERED: there being good cause pursuant to CCP 2032.320 (a) the motion for the Independent Psychiatric and Psychological Examination is GRANTED in part and the Plaintiff shall submit to the examinations the location of which to be addressed at the hearing. The Parties are ORDERED to APPEAR at the hearing to address CCP 2032.320 (e) and whether there is good cause for the travel involved. The court notes the Plaintiff's position that the Plaintiff resides in Santa Monica and his support system is in Santa Monica. The Plaintiff states that it will be traumatic for the Plaintiff to travel to San Francisco and the court is sensitive to that. The court does recognize that the Plaintiff has to travel to San Francisco for the trial and so travel to San Francisco related to this matter cannot be avoided should the matter go to trial. The Defendant has submitted a declaration and their intent to proffer $2,000 to the Plaintiff for reasonable costs and expenses related to the travel per CCP 2032.320 (e)(2) however the issue of whether there is good cause for the travel remains. The Defendant has the right to choose the experts to perform the examinations. The question is should the Plaintiff be required to travel for the examination or should the experts be required to travel for the examination. The court recognizes that the Plaintiff's treating doctors are all in the Los Angeles area and will presumably travel to San Francisco for trial. The Defendants stated experts will not have to travel for the trial. Defendant please be prepared to address why the Ordered examinations cannot take place in the Santa Monica area. Plaintiff please be prepared to address why the Plaintiff cannot travel to San Francisco for the examinations given that he may travel to San Francisco for the trial, and whether the $2,000 advancement of expenses is reasonable. (end of part 1 of 2) =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: JASON ROUSSOS VS. POUR GUYS, INC. ET AL

The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. (end part 2 of 2) =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: KIM MITCHELL VS. WELLS FARGO BANK, NA, A CORPORATION ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Wells Fargo'S Motion For Terminating And Monetary Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Thursday, September 13, 2018, Line 5, DEFENDANT WELLS FARGO BANK, NA,'S Motion For Terminating And Monetary Sanctions. Pro Tem Judge Dianne Peebles, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Defendant Wells Fargo Bank's Notice Of Motion And Motion For Terminating And Monetary Sanctions having been duly considered and no opposition thereto it is hereby ORDERED: there being good cause the Motion is GRANTED and pursuant to CCP 2023.030 (d) (2) the Plaintiff is stayed from any further proceedings until the discovery order of July 19, 2018 is obeyed. Additional monetary sanctions are awarded to the moving party Defendant Wells Fargo in the amount of $1,000 and Plaintiff is to pay those sanctions by October 1, 2018. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to dpeebleslaw@comcast.net with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: DAVID HOPPER ET AL VS. ANDY TRAN
rulings.Calendar Matter: Defendant Andy Tran'S Notice Of Motion And Motion To Dismiss Or Stay For Forum Non Conveniens
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, September 13, 2018, Line 6, Defendant Andy Tran'S Motion To Dismiss Or Stay For Forum Non Conveniens Defendant Tran's motion to dismiss is GRANTED. The court dismisses this action. The mandatory forum selection clause indicates that the parties should litigate this dispute in Delaware, the parties currently are engaged in litigation in that state, and there is no opposition filed. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4 pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. Counsel for Tran is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. =302/acm
